transcript.text: Good, good.
What do you mean you left me hanging?

Okay, okay.
I didn't even notice the time, so no worries.
You're still, we're still the first on the call.
the first.
I was just meeting with Elvis to kind of get up to speed on his clients, so that's the reason I'm running a little late here, but things are moving and grooving quite quickly.
Yeah, yeah, I know.
Yeah, how's your day been going so far?
It's good.
Yeah, it's good.
I had a couple calls with people for a few roles, unfortunately not for Freelancer or CM, but like, I know Matthew, anyway, it's a continual update every day.
It's like, no real update, but yeah, so that was kind of nice.
And other than that.
Yeah, nothing too crazy yet.
So that's kind of good.
Just getting up to speed on the new clients.
How about you?
How's your day going?
Pretty good.
Just like, I'm trying to prepare as much as possible.
So the guys have enough for next week.
So yeah, I was just doing a little review of prophecy.
I'm not happy with the performance there.
So yeah, it's like, just trying to see like, where there's something short.
Yeah, yeah, just slated a bunch of refreshes there.
Hopefully, you that should pick this back up.
So yeah, when you're out, do you typically go async that week for with the clients?
Or like, what's the standard kind of operation there?
So far, I've never been off for an entire week.
Nice.
Like, calls or whatever.
When that happens, one of the guys either steps up, or Kyle...
is to lead the call.
So my guess is for Promful, to be honest, I think, you know, if you have the time, I think you can probably lead that call.
Sure.
For Galileo, I think, again, might go async or, you know, Nika knows what's going on in this account, but he's never actually sort of let her call on Galileo.
So I think Mike always think that.

Like, he knows what's going on, but I don't think he's up to leading the call.
Like last time I asked him, even for Promful, he kind of, he was like, okay, that's not.
Yeah.
All right.
Hey, Conor.
Hey, Conor.
How's it going?
Going well.
How about you?
Not too shabby, yeah.
Busy start to the day.
So again, yeah, apologies about the late.
Jump, but I'm excited to connect with you both.
Yeah.
Morris, you can go right in.
Mara, where are you based on?
I don't remember.
I'm in Crown Heights.
I'm in Brooklyn.
Awesome.
I'm in the Seattle area.
Oh, lovely.
Okay, great.
I feel like every time, I know there's a ton of rain, but every time I see a photo of anywhere in Seattle, I get a little existential dread.
I'm like, should I be there?
Look at all that.
No, mean, New York's fantastic.
So I think Seattle's got its high points, but the winters are tough, I will say.
We're starting to hit the part of the year where I'm like, oh, I love it here.
Then by next December, Mara and I will be like, oh, man, why are we here again?
I totally hear you.
I'm honestly, we're not quite there in New York, but we will be soon, where you get that like kind of selective amnesia, you know, where you're like, oh, it's great year round.
Yeah.
Cool.
Awesome.
Sorry, Vivek.
Let you take us through the performance.
Talking about Lisbon and makes you look really jealous.
Yeah.
Yeah.
I can't have you talk about this one because I will give her a job.
Have mercy.
Yeah.
Well, all right.
So we're jumping into the updates.
we have a lot of schedule this week.
Two of them are the competitor articles, Conor.
So what we've done is we've just cleared a template of that, and we're just kind of running our flows through it.
This is what we get.
Did Jason ever give you access to those docs, by the way?
No, I still don't have access.
Let me bother him.
Or forget here.
Thank you.
Thank you.
Cool.
Just noticed something about that.
Let's try to get you some more info.
Quick context for you, by the way, is that we are going to go through a brief product marketing transition here.
So Merdiza is leaving the team.
So we're going to potentially have to redo some of our competitor docs here in a few weeks.
We don't have someone new coming in yet.
So it shouldn't really impact us too much, but it may just slow down a couple of our internal messaging updates to you.
Great.
So yeah, in terms of refreshes, we're looking at just prioritizing the top performing content that should start from next week.

And then regarding last week's conversation about the scope extension, know Mara and I are in discussions about just expanding that and also working in the work stream that you and Jason have discussed with the team in person as well.
Should we have an update on that for you?
Yeah.
It was a super, and then my follow-up.
With Shohill regarding the tracking with an app spot, I'll just keep thinking about that.
then the social fake queues, I had, Jen and I went back and forth a bit.
Basically, at the end of it, I still know that for that blog post, we kind of need one and a half to two weeks' notice because there will be a one-week week time of recreating it and review it.
So moving on to the performance stage, we saw a jump in clicks this week, which we were kind of expecting given the...
jump in impressions over the previous weeks, so pretty good news over there.
And then looking at the categories themselves, most of them are performing quite well, to be honest.
even stuff that's, you know, in the 14th or 13th position or even 12th position, that's like the average.
So, you know, it just means that a lot of articles are ranking high.
Generally, we're happy to look at these numbers.
We're going to use higher CTRs in some of these things.
And that's kind of...
where we're diving in a bit just to see like we're trying to use some information or updates, etc.
Generally, LLM eval sort of tends to, it's still attracting a lot of clicks.
And we, you know, that's one of the reasons why we work our hours a bit.
They also happen to be our oldest article, so they can definitely use those clicks.
In terms of looking at the LLM referral traffic, we had a nice month this week.
Most of it came from VTT again.
A lot of questions seem to be coming towards just how to test AI engines.
Yeah, exactly.
Yeah, we had refreshed that article and what we noticed is post-refresh, this happened I think sometime last month, post-refresh, it's been the LLM referrals pretty massive.
So, generally, things are looking good on the referral side of things as well.
Unfortunately, at this point, there's not...
Much more we can dig into this because it's kind of like, you know, the black box a bit.
Yeah.
Yeah.
So generally, that's pretty much it.
So for next week, we'll just be focusing on the processes as well as fixing them up with the existing article and topics that we have fixed.
We have some AI security topics coming out as well.
So those might be a bit more technical, but it'll be interesting to see if we get the same performance from them.
I mean, just on a more personal note, I will be out of office next week, but Mara's here, Smiles here, Luke is here as well.
So there's pretty good coverage in that regard.
And yes, that's going to be the update for this week.
A couple of thoughts here.
So one, so we're just over 400, just under 500 clicks last week, correct, based on this chart?
Yeah.
What?
What?
So I'm starting to work on my goal setting for RQ2, which is May through July.
What would you project as where you think we can be on a weekly basis by end of July, based on continuing the program, shipping eight articles a week, and maybe making some technical improvements?
I think we're at $420 right now.
I think conservatively, I'd say between $650 to $700 is a good target.
That's being conservative.
Unless, of course, there are some other stretch goals that you wouldn't like to do.
I mean, I love a round number.
So in my head, I'm like, oh, a thousand clicks a week would be fantastic.
Fantastic.
And looking at the trend line, I know we've slowed down a little bit on trajectory, but we've essentially gone from zero to 500 clicks, or will have, hopefully, by the end of April.
So I guess I'm wondering if it's realistic to project a double, or if you think we should be a little more conservative, given the age of articles.
I would be a little more conservative, yes, because competition is going to increase, and then I'm just more conservative, generally.
So it's just my personal pick.
I'd like to hit a thousand, and I don't think it's completely out of the question.
But yeah, to be conservative, I'd say between 612 to 700 is my nice target date.
I like 700 as a goal.
That makes sense to me, where we're still being relatively aggressive, but also not too out of whack.
Okay, let just take a quick note on this.
Fathom, Fathom, Fathom, Fathom, Fathom, Fathom.
Fathom, Fathom, Fathom, Fathom, Fathom, Fathom, Fathom, Fathom, Fathom, Fathom, Fathom, Fathom, Fathom, F
Maybe $6.50 with seasonality.
I'll have to look at that.
Awesome.
Thank you.
And then I'm curious, I mean, obviously, this is less of a directly aligned to what we're doing as far as the LLM referral traffic.
We want to jump over to that chart really quickly.
I want to set a goal for that, too.
This one's more variable.
So we're up to 74.
we're at 74 sessions right now.
And I think the trend here is actually a little more, you know, I don't want to say exponential, but it's definitely more of a curve.
By the end of July, I've seen, I can double this by end of July.
Okay.
So maybe around 150, 160, somewhere.
I'm going to say 140 to give us a little room here, but I like your thought process, yeah.
Great.
That's helpful.
I'm curious if there are any other goals.
Since your main goal is just awareness, I would say internally, you're tracking branded searches, that would be a good benchmark to him.

Tying in with the questions I had regarding HubSpot, I don't know if any of those tie into specifically brand awareness goals, but it might be good to take a look at those, and then we might get some additional metrics.
So you're thinking like a number of conversions off of article-generated traffic?
Yeah, it's conversion to the sense of how many are downloading your e-books, revisiting the demo page, and those particular metrics.
Those are basically our CQAs at the end of the article, so even over there would be good.
Perfect.
Awesome.
Mara, I'm curious if there's other thoughts from your end.
I know you're still...
Somewhat in the midst of onboarding here, but yeah, curious if you have any insights for us.
No, I just, I agree with Vivek.
I think maybe in the future, too, it could be interesting.
You know, actually, we're building out a few more workflows, and so I want to see how those develop before offering them here.
But I think just in terms of awareness, there's a lot that we could do.
But no, I think short term, this is a really good way of thinking about it.
I'll share as soon as we start developing something else in a way that we can pilot here as well.
Great.
And Vivek, my understanding is y'all are looking at a couple different tools for backend numbers.
So obviously, we've got this Looker Report.
I know y'all are using SEMrush, and then I forget the other one you're using as well.
But there's a couple different tools you're looking at, right?
You're a looker.
We are looking at SEMrush.
Oh, sorry.
mean, I meant internally, I believe you are.
Leveraging SEMrush, though, as far as your internal tooling.
Maybe I'm going to correct you.
Yeah, for the keyword research.
Cool.
As far as topic clusters go right now, we have a good amount of stuff ready to go.
Do you have a projection for when we'll want to update topic clusters again?
I would update the topic clusters only if the product marketing focus changes, because I think they're pretty well aligned with what Galileo does.
still have a depth to go into.
Yeah, I don't see a need to update those.
Great.
That's all I had from my end.
I know we've got about 20 articles in our view, most of which are podcast ones.
Is there anything else top of mind that I should be keying in on that maybe I'm missing?
Um...
Nothing urgent.
The main thing is, if you could just look at the review articles or versus articles, there'll be a couple on the street.
Yes, yeah.
The feedback regarding the data in there, the format as well, I'm getting a lot of points.
I mean, things are pretty good.
Perfect.
Well, thank you both so much.
Really appreciate the time and attention here.
And yeah, we'll prioritize those versus articles as soon as they're up and ready to go.
Awesome.
You too.
Thank you.
Have a good one.
Yeah.
Bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Weekly sync between Galileo and GrowthX teams to discuss content performance, goals, and next steps.](https://fathom.video/share/JyuRsUzNF59zGwpev1zg8sA6ihzpsTzB?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Content performance is strong: clicks jumped to \~420/week, LLM referrals at 74 sessions](https://fathom.video/share/JyuRsUzNF59zGwpev1zg8sA6ihzpsTzB?tab=summary&timestamp=420.0)
  - [Q2 goals set: 650-700 weekly clicks, 140 LLM referrals by July](https://fathom.video/share/JyuRsUzNF59zGwpev1zg8sA6ihzpsTzB?tab=summary&timestamp=567.0)
  - [Focus on refreshing top-performing content, competitor articles, and AI security topics](https://fathom.video/share/JyuRsUzNF59zGwpev1zg8sA6ihzpsTzB?tab=summary&timestamp=248.0)
  - [Product marketing transition may impact internal messaging updates](https://fathom.video/share/JyuRsUzNF59zGwpev1zg8sA6ihzpsTzB?tab=summary&timestamp=322.0)

## Topics

### Content Performance Update

  - [Clicks jumped this week, aligning with previous impression increases](https://fathom.video/share/JyuRsUzNF59zGwpev1zg8sA6ihzpsTzB?tab=summary&timestamp=382.0)
  - [Most categories performing well, even at lower positions (12th-14th)](https://fathom.video/share/JyuRsUzNF59zGwpev1zg8sA6ihzpsTzB?tab=summary&timestamp=401.0)
  - [LLM eval content still attracting significant clicks](https://fathom.video/share/JyuRsUzNF59zGwpev1zg8sA6ihzpsTzB?tab=summary&timestamp=428.0)
  - [LLM referral traffic had a strong month, mostly from VTT](https://fathom.video/share/JyuRsUzNF59zGwpev1zg8sA6ihzpsTzB?tab=summary&timestamp=447.0)
  - [Refreshed "how to test AI engines" article driving massive LLM referrals](https://fathom.video/share/JyuRsUzNF59zGwpev1zg8sA6ihzpsTzB?tab=summary&timestamp=460.0)

### Q2 Goal Setting

  - [Current: \~420 weekly clicks](https://fathom.video/share/JyuRsUzNF59zGwpev1zg8sA6ihzpsTzB?tab=summary&timestamp=529.0)
  - [Conservative Q2 goal: 650-700 weekly clicks by end of July](https://fathom.video/share/JyuRsUzNF59zGwpev1zg8sA6ihzpsTzB?tab=summary&timestamp=567.0)
  - [Ambitious target: 1000 weekly clicks (deemed possible but less likely)](https://fathom.video/share/JyuRsUzNF59zGwpev1zg8sA6ihzpsTzB?tab=summary&timestamp=584.0)
  - [LLM referral goal: 140 sessions/week by end of July (up from 74)](https://fathom.video/share/JyuRsUzNF59zGwpev1zg8sA6ihzpsTzB?tab=summary&timestamp=704.0)
  - [Additional goals to consider: branded searches, HubSpot conversions (e-book downloads, demo page visits)](https://fathom.video/share/JyuRsUzNF59zGwpev1zg8sA6ihzpsTzB?tab=summary&timestamp=723.0)

### Content Strategy

  - [Prioritizing refreshes for top-performing content](https://fathom.video/share/JyuRsUzNF59zGwpev1zg8sA6ihzpsTzB?tab=summary&timestamp=332.0)
  - [Two competitor articles scheduled this week](https://fathom.video/share/JyuRsUzNF59zGwpev1zg8sA6ihzpsTzB?tab=summary&timestamp=253.0)
  - [AI security topics in pipeline (more technical)](https://fathom.video/share/JyuRsUzNF59zGwpev1zg8sA6ihzpsTzB?tab=summary&timestamp=500.0)
  - [No immediate need to update topic clusters unless product marketing focus changes](https://fathom.video/share/JyuRsUzNF59zGwpev1zg8sA6ihzpsTzB?tab=summary&timestamp=867.0)
  - [\~20 articles in queue, mostly podcast-related](https://fathom.video/share/JyuRsUzNF59zGwpev1zg8sA6ihzpsTzB?tab=summary&timestamp=892.0)

### Team Updates

  - [Vivek out of office next week; Mara, Smile, and Luke covering](https://fathom.video/share/JyuRsUzNF59zGwpev1zg8sA6ihzpsTzB?tab=summary&timestamp=510.0)
  - [Product marketing transition: Merdiza leaving, may slow down internal messaging updates](https://fathom.video/share/JyuRsUzNF59zGwpev1zg8sA6ihzpsTzB?tab=summary&timestamp=314.0)

### Operational Notes

  - [Conor still needs access to competitor article docs](https://fathom.video/share/JyuRsUzNF59zGwpev1zg8sA6ihzpsTzB?tab=summary&timestamp=264.0)
  - [1.5-2 weeks' notice needed for social media queue blog posts](https://fathom.video/share/JyuRsUzNF59zGwpev1zg8sA6ihzpsTzB?tab=summary&timestamp=370.0)
  - [Exploring HubSpot tracking capabilities for additional metrics](https://fathom.video/share/JyuRsUzNF59zGwpev1zg8sA6ihzpsTzB?tab=summary&timestamp=723.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Conor to follow up on access to competitor article docs](https://fathom.video/share/JyuRsUzNF59zGwpev1zg8sA6ihzpsTzB?tab=summary&timestamp=269.0)
  - [Team to focus on refreshing existing content and competitor articles](https://fathom.video/share/JyuRsUzNF59zGwpev1zg8sA6ihzpsTzB?tab=summary&timestamp=332.0)
  - [Vivek/Mara to provide update on scope extension and new work stream](https://fathom.video/share/JyuRsUzNF59zGwpev1zg8sA6ihzpsTzB?tab=summary&timestamp=339.0)
  - [Conor to prioritize review of "versus" articles when available](https://fathom.video/share/JyuRsUzNF59zGwpev1zg8sA6ihzpsTzB?tab=summary&timestamp=900.0)
  - [Team to continue monitoring performance against new Q2 goals](https://fathom.video/share/JyuRsUzNF59zGwpev1zg8sA6ihzpsTzB?tab=summary&timestamp=899.0)

action_items.description: Bother Jason for access to competitor docs
Review HubSpot metrics for brand awareness goals (e-book downloads, demo page visits)
Prioritize review of versus articles when ready, focus on data and format
